page: Amazon Web Services c5n.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. The instance provides 36 dedicated vCPUs across 18 physical cores, with 2 threads per core. It includes 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 2.67 GB of memory per core. Cache specifications consist of a 32 KB L1d cache, a 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a 24 MB L3 cache. The CPU supports AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets, alongside AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA capabilities. Local storage is not bundled, and there are no GPUs.

The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results place its performance consistently in the average (middle 50%) tier across multiple workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1425.89, while multi-core performance reaches 33340.81. Memory bandwidth tests show 1853.99 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 104.3 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and 45.03 GB/sec for large-block writes. In database operations, the instance achieves a PassMark database score of 12260.61 and processes 32.41 million Redis SET operations per second. Static web serving throughput is recorded at 21.71 GB/sec.

While the c5n.9xlarge handles small to medium language model inference adequately, achieving 1846.91 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M model and 53.23 tokens per second on a 7B model, it exhibits a significant performance tradeoff on larger models. Prompt processing for a 70B LLM drops to 5.27 tokens per second, which is categorized in the poor (bottom 10%) range. Given the lack of local storage and GPUs, combined with high network bandwidth and compute density, this instance is suited for network-bound compute workloads, distributed compilation, static web serving, and database operations rather than large-scale AI model execution.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.9xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8124M processor operating at 3.4 GHz, delivering 36 dedicated vCPUs across 18 physical cores. The system includes 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a multi-core stress-ng score of 33340.81 and a static web serving throughput of 21.71 GB/sec. However, a notable tradeoff is observed in large-scale language model inference, where prompt processing for a 70B model falls to a poor 5.27 tokens per second. This instance is suited for network-intensive compute tasks, software compilation, and database operations that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.9xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 36 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 36 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed networking and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for network-bound and distributed workloads.
bullet_points: 36 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor at 3.4 GHz
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps
Average CPU performance with a multi-core stress-ng score of 33340.81
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Poor performance on large-scale LLM inference workloads
Suited for network-bound compute, static web serving, and database operations
